items.title: Bitcoin, ether little changed as U.S. launches fresh Iran strikes
items.description: The U.S. hit Iran for the third time this week and Tehran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz again.

items.title: Ripple once weighed shutting down and handing XRP to shareholders, CEO says
items.description: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse says he and co-founder Chris Larsen considered winding the company down and handing its XRP to shareholders before deciding to fight the 2020 lawsuit.

items.title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
items.description: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.

items.title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
items.description: Bonzo Lend lost approximately $9.05 million after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract on the Hedera network.

items.title: Crypto IPO market stalls as capital rotates to AI and macro uncertainty weighs
items.description: Funding constraints and investor caution, not regulation, are delaying crypto IPOs, according to Cohen & Company Capital Markets' Christian Lopez.

items.title: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
items.description: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.

items.title: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital sold about half of its BTC stack
items.description: It's a sign of the times as the troubled company swaps its bitcoin treasury ambitions for AI data centers.

items.title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
items.description: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Bitcoin bulls Michael Saylor, Adam Back slam BIP-110 Ordinals proposal
items.description: The ongoing debate comes despite a broad downturn in Ordinals transaction activity over the last two years.

items.title: Empery Digital shares rise after selling Bitcoin to fund AI data center project
items.description: The sales come months after a major Empery shareholder demanded the firm ditch its Bitcoin treasury strategy and force the resignation of its CEO and board.

items.title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision
items.description: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts said it's far too early to call $1 million by 2030, but he's confident Bitcoin could climb up to $250,000 over the next couple of years.

items.title: Ethereum climbs 3% on tokenization boom: Can bulls push ETH price past $1,800?
items.description: Despite strong tokenization gains and institutional accumulation, weak onchain and derivatives data leaves ETH vulnerable to a $1,700 retest.

items.title: Bonzo Lend loses $9M in oracle exploit on Hedera
items.description: An attacker inflated the value of SAUCE collateral and borrowed $9 million from Bonzo Lend through a flaw in Supra’s onchain oracle verifier.

items.title: Bitcoin price gains nearly 10% in July, but traders still see BTC copying 2022 bear market
items.description: Bitcoin price gains approached 10% for the first two weeks of the month, but analysis warned of the bear market returning from August onward.

items.title: Dollar stablecoins could improve FX access but amplify currency runs: IMF paper
items.description: An IMF working paper says dollar stablecoins can improve access to foreign currency but may also help coordinate exits from local currencies during periods of severe exchange-rate stress.

items.title: U.S. launches airstrikes against Iran after Tehran attacks container ship in Hormuz, Pentagon says
items.description: Iran has attacked ships using a route along Oman's coast protected by the U.S. military. Tehran demands vessels use a northern route through its waters.

items.title: 'Funflation' hits home: Why staying in isn't the cost-saver it used to be
items.description: Following a wave of price hikes, at-home pastimes like streaming movies or playing video games are pinching pocketbooks.

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI alleging trade secret theft, says scheme was 'at every level'
items.description: The two companies entered into a high-profile partnership in 2024 when ChatGPT was integrated into the iPhone's operating system.

items.title: Burnout, frustration and heartbreak: Amazon layoffs take their toll in saturated job market
items.description: In the eight-plus months since Amazon announced its most expansive job cuts ever, laid off workers have been thrust into an increasingly saturated labor market.

items.title: New housing law targets affordability — what it means for homebuyers and sellers
items.description: While the legislation is aimed at least partly at addressing affordability, experts say the benefits of the measure will take time to show up in the market.

items.title: These underperforming trades could yield big returns over next six months
items.description: ETF Action's Mike Akins is encouraging investors to boost exposure to groups that underperformed compared with major artificial intelligence stocks.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway gains ground, but still trails the S&P 500 as '26 enters second half
items.description: With 2026 a bit more than half over, Berkshire Hathaway's B shares are down 1.8% year-to-date and 12.4 percentage points behind the S&P 500's 10.7% gain.

items.title: A tiny GLP-1 implant is the latest bet to help patients maintain their weight loss
items.description: Vivani Medical is developing an implant of semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk's obesity injection Wegovy and diabetes counterpart Ozempic.

items.title: Bitcoin holds gains above $64,000 as U.S. crypto policy remains in focus

items.title: Stranded pollock prompt EU to delay fish import controls for US
items.description: System introduced to crack down on illegal fishing has left cargoes stuck in docks for days

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VtI5Og0ktok! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: My insurance company said my roof lost a few tiles. Loss adjusters found $10,000 in storm damage. How could this happen?
items.description: “My house shook violently from the wind.”

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

items.title: Is it better to spend my savings now so I can delay taking Social Security? How do I choose?
items.description: “By claiming earlier, I could preserve more of my portfolio and allow those assets to continue compounding.”

items.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity
items.description: Your share of the AI wealth is a right — not a handout. Here is how we claw back our money.

items.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
items.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. Your retirement plan might disagree.
items.description: More investors want their money to reflect their values. It feels righteous — but the reality is a lot messier.

items.title: The dot-com crash was a $5 trillion blip. Why the next financial crisis could hit 4 times harder.
items.description: The next global financial crisis is already under way. Here is how it will unfold.

1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies, aged 71
items.description: South Carolina politician was one of the most influential and hawkish Republicans

items.title: US launches more strikes after Tehran declares Strait of Hormuz closed
items.description: Iran hits commercial ship transiting the waterway, with Centcom saying crew member is missing from vessel

items.title: UK launches national dementia registry to speed up search for treatments
items.description: Platform aims to improve access to clinical trials and boost life sciences investment in the country

items.title: Stranded pollock prompt EU to delay fish import controls for US
items.description: System introduced to crack down on illegal fishing has left cargoes stuck in docks for days

items.title: The ‘innovators and disrupters’ hired to bring AI to the UK public sector
items.description: Government fellowship scheme places tech experts on ‘high-impact tours of duty’ to improve services

items.title: Big Tech to face fines for consumer protection failures, says EU official
items.description: Justice commissioner Michael McGrath says Brussels seeks to strengthen social media safeguards

items.title: The EU has ways out of its budget trap
items.description: Side agreements and special purpose funds could bypass the usual trench warfare

items.title: Airbnb lures fans from US hotels after World Cup marketing push
items.description: Hotel bookings fall short of expectations as price-conscious fans opt to stay in American homes

items.title: How Putin Turned Japan Into a Den of Spies
items.description: Operating out of a Tokyo high-rise, a military intelligence unit finds the high-tech equipment that Russia needs to wage war.

items.title: Inside the Secret Factory That Supplies Ukraine’s War Drones
items.description: With its mass-produced and inexpensive A.I. powered war machines, the German start-up Helsing SE illustrates a profound shift in military spending.

items.title: Trump Accounts Prompt Parents to Start Saving Early for Their Kids
items.description: Between Trump accounts and child-friendly investment platforms, it has become much easier for parents to invest for their children’s future.

items.title: Kansas City, the Smallest World Cup Host, Has the World at Its Feet
items.description: The city has a proud history with the sport thanks to Lamar Hunt, who owned the Kansas City Chiefs and co-founded Major League Soccer.

items.title: What Slate Auto’s $25,000 Electric Pickup Truck Is Like in Person
items.description: The Slate Auto truck is making a splash for its lack of frills and its relatively affordable price, but it’s jumping into a difficult market.

items.title: What’s in the Housing Bill That Just Became Law
items.description: It is no Great Society measure, but the new law offers local governments and builders incentives to make incremental changes.

items.title: Kids Can’t Stop Watching ‘Moana.’ There’s a Scientific Explanation.
items.description: The 2016 release has become the most watched movie on Disney+. Parents and experts explain why kids can’t get enough.

items.title: Times Journalists Subpoenaed as Trump Escalates Pressure on Media
items.description: The Justice Department is seeking to compel testimony from reporters who wrote about the new Air Force One. The Times called the move a “brazen act.”
